home.title: Qualification and Selection Of Open Source Software
home.subtitle: Your ultimate guide to navigate the vast world of open source solutions
home.make_choice: Be sure to make the right choice
home.make_choice_desc: QSOS is a comprehensive method designed to help you effectively evaluate and select the best open source software for your projects.
home.pool_effort_desc: QSOS is also a collaborative project aimed at pooling and capitalizing on technological monitoring of open source components and projects.

about.title: About QSOS
about.tool_description: QSOS (Qualification and Selection of Open Source software) is a tool that allows you to evaluate, qualify and compare open source software using a structured and transparent methodology.

qsos_maturity_2_0.maturity_desc: Maturity of the project in charge of the product's development and maintenance
qsos_maturity_2_0.age_desc1: between three months and three years
qsos_maturity_2_0.history_desc0: The software has many problems which can be prohibitive
qsos_maturity_2_0.history_desc1: No major crisis, or unknown history
qsos_maturity_2_0.history_desc2: Good past experience in crisis management
qsos_maturity_2_0.activity_desc: Activity inside and around the project
qsos_maturity_2_0.contributing_community_desc0: No real community nor activity (forum, mailing list...)
qsos_maturity_2_0.contributing_community_desc2: Strong community with vivid activity in forums, with many contributors and supporters
qsos_maturity_2_0.activity_on_bugs_desc0: Low reactivity in forums and mailing lists, or no mention about bugfixes in release notes
qsos_maturity_2_0.activity_on_bugs_desc1: Existing activity but without any clearly defined process or with long resolution times
qsos_maturity_2_0.activity_on_bugs_desc2: Strong reactivity based on roles and task assignments
qsos_maturity_2_0.activity_on_functionalities_desc1: Product's evolution is led by a dedicated team or by users, but without a clearly stated process
qsos_maturity_2_0.activity_on_functionalities_desc2: Feature request process is industrialized, an associated roadmap is available
qsos_maturity_2_0.activity_on_releases_desc0: Very low activity on the production or development versions (alpha, beta)
qsos_maturity_2_0.activity_on_releases_desc1: Activity on production or development versions (alpha, beta), with frequent minor corrective versions
qsos_maturity_2_0.activity_on_releases_desc2: Important activity with frequent corrective versions and planned major versions linked with the roadmap
qsos_maturity_2_0.copyright_holders_desc0: Rights are held by a few individuals or commercial entities
qsos_maturity_2_0.copyright_holders_desc1: Rights are uniformly held by many individuals
qsos_maturity_2_0.copyright_holders_desc2: Rights are held by a legal entity or a foundation that the community trust (eg FSF, Apache, ObjectWeb)
qsos_maturity_2_0.roadmap_desc2: Versioned roadmap with planning and delay measurements
qsos_maturity_2_0.project_management_desc0: No clear and apparent project management
qsos_maturity_2_0.project_management_desc1: Project managed by an individual or a single commercial entity
qsos_maturity_2_0.project_management_desc2: strong independence of core team, rights held by a recognized entity
qsos_maturity_2_0.distribution_mode_desc0: Dual distribution with a commercial version along with a functionally limited free one
qsos_maturity_2_0.distribution_mode_desc1: Subparts are only available under a proprietary license (core, plugins...)
qsos_maturity_2_0.services_desc1: Limited service offering (geographically, to a single language, to a single provider or without warranty)
qsos_maturity_2_0.services_desc2: Rich ecosystem of services provided by multiple suppliers, with guaranteed results
qsos_maturity_2_0.documentation_desc1: Documentation exists but is partly obsolete or restricted to one language or to few details
qsos_maturity_2_0.documentation_desc2: Documentation up to date, translated and possibly adapted to several target readers (enduser, sysadmin, manager...)
qsos_maturity_2_0.quality_assurance_desc1: Existing QA processes, but they are not formalized or equipped
qsos_maturity_2_0.quality_assurance_desc2: QA process based on standard tools and methodologies
qsos_maturity_2_0.source_code_modification_desc0: No convenient way to propose source code modifications
qsos_maturity_2_0.source_code_modification_desc1: Tools are provided to access and modify the code (eg SCM, forge...) but are not really used by core team to develop the product
qsos_maturity_2_0.source_code_modification_desc2: The contributing process is well defined, exposed and respected, it is based on clearly defined roles
